ins: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Indian Sign Language.
ion: noun. An atom or group of atoms bearing an electrical charge, such as the sodium and chlorine atoms in a salt solution.
ism: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Masimasi.
miosis: noun. Contraction of the pupil of the eye.
mis: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for unencoded languages.
miso: noun. A thick paste made by fermentation of soybeans with the mold Aspergillus oryzae, used in making soups and sauces.
miss: verb. To fail to hit, catch, grasp, etc.
mission: noun. A set of tasks that fulfills a purpose or duty; an assignment set by an employer, or by oneself.
mon: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mongolian.
mono: noun. A bicycle or motorcycle trick where the front wheel is lifted off the ground while riding.
mons: noun. One of the fleshy areas at the base of the fingers; a mount.
moo: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Monom.
moon: proper noun. Alternative letter-case form of Moon (“the Earth 's only permanent natural satellite”).
mos: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Mooré.
moss: noun. Any of various small, green, seedless plants growing on the ground or on the surfaces of trees, stones, etc.; now specifically, a
nim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nilamba.
nims: verb. third-person singular simple present indicative of nim
nom: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Nocamán.
nomos: noun. The body of law, especially that governing human behaviour.
nos: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Eastern Nisu.
ons: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Ono.
sim: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Mende (New Guinea).
sis: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Siuslaw.
son: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-5 language code for Songhay languages.
soon: adjective. Short in length of time from the present.
sos: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Seenku.
